name: Agile and Engineering Practices
description: Agile delivery, Scrum, Kanban, estimation, testing, collaboration, leadership and continuous improvement.

topics.name: Agile Mindset
topics.description: Short feedback loops and adaptive delivery rather than a meeting schedule
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Agile is a set of values for delivering useful software under uncertainty through working increments, collaboration and adaptation.

## What problem does it solve?

Long plans can efficiently deliver the wrong product when assumptions are not tested.

## How it works

- Deliver small usable increments.
- Collect feedback early.
- Preserve a clear goal while adapting the plan.
- Maintain technical quality and sustainable pace.

## Common mistakes

- Treating Agile as ceremonies.
- Using Agile as an excuse for no design or documentation.
- Accepting constant scope drift.

## Key terms

- feedback loop — time from decision to evidence
- increment — usable product addition
- sustainable pace — delivery without permanent overtime

hu.name: Agile Mindset
hu.description: Rövid feedback loop és adaptive delivery, nem egyszerű meetinglista
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Az Agile értékek gyűjteménye useful software szállítására working increment, collaboration és adaptation segítségével.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A hosszú terv rossz productot is szállíthat, ha az assumptionök túl későn kapnak feedbacket.

## Hogyan működik?

- Kis usable incrementet szállíts.
- Korán gyűjts feedbacket.
- A clear goal maradjon, a plan legyen adaptálható.
- Technical quality és sustainable pace szükséges.

## Gyakori hibák

- Az Agile-t ceremonykkal azonosítani.
- Design és documentation hiányát Agile-lal indokolni.
- Folyamatos scope driftet elfogadni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- feedback loop — döntés és evidence közötti idő
- increment — usable product addition
- sustainable pace — permanent overtime nélküli delivery

topics.name: Scrum Framework
topics.description: Sprint-based product inspection with clear accountabilities and goals
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Scrum is a lightweight framework for complex product work using a Product Goal, short Sprints and regular inspection.

## What problem does it solve?

It creates focus and a predictable feedback cadence without pretending the full solution is known upfront.

## How it works

- Product Owner orders the backlog.
- Scrum Master enables effective Scrum.
- Developers create the Increment.
- Planning, Daily Scrum, Review and Retrospective inspect and adapt work.

## Common mistakes

- Scrum Master as task manager.
- Daily Scrum as management report.
- Review as slides instead of product inspection.

## Key terms

- Product Goal — longer-term objective
- Sprint Goal — objective for one Sprint
- Increment — usable Done work

hu.name: Scrum Framework
hu.description: Sprint-alapú product inspection tiszta accountabilityvel és goalokkal
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Scrum lightweight framework komplex product workhöz Product Goal, rövid Sprint és regular inspection segítségével.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Focus-t és predictable feedback cadence-t ad anélkül, hogy a teljes solutiont előre ismertnek tekintené.

## Hogyan működik?

- Product Owner ordereli a backlogot.
- Scrum Master támogatja a framework működését.
- Developers készíti az Incrementet.
- Planning, Daily, Review és Retrospective inspectál és adaptál.

## Gyakori hibák

- Scrum Mastert task managerként kezelni.
- Daily-t management reporttá tenni.
- Review-n working product helyett slide-ot mutatni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- Product Goal — hosszabb távú objective
- Sprint Goal — egy Sprint objective-je
- Increment — usable Done work

topics.name: Kanban
topics.description: Continuous flow with visualized work and WIP limits
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Kanban improves flow by visualizing work, limiting work in progress and making policies explicit.

## What problem does it solve?

Too much concurrent work creates waiting, context switching and hidden bottlenecks.

## How it works

- Model the real workflow.
- Set WIP limits.
- Pull work when capacity is available.
- Track lead time, cycle time, throughput and cumulative flow.

## Common mistakes

- A board without WIP limits.
- Every request marked urgent.
- Blocked work kept invisible.

## Key terms

- WIP limit — maximum active work
- pull system — work starts when capacity exists
- cycle time — started to done

hu.name: Kanban
hu.description: Continuous flow visualized work és WIP limit mellett
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Kanban visualizálja a worköt, limitálja a work in progresst és explicit policykkal javítja a flow-t.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A túl sok concurrent work waitinget, context switchinget és hidden bottlenecket okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- A valós workflow-t modellezd.
- Használj WIP limitet.
- Capacity esetén pullolj új worköt.
- Mérj lead time-ot, cycle time-ot, throughputot és cumulative flow-t.

## Gyakori hibák

- WIP limit nélküli board.
- Minden request urgent.
- Blocked work elrejtése.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- WIP limit — maximum active work
- pull system — capacity esetén induló work
- cycle time — startedtől done-ig

topics.name: SDLC Delivery Models
topics.description: Sequential, iterative, incremental and risk-driven delivery choices
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

SDLC models order requirements, design, implementation, verification and release differently.

## What problem does it solve?

Stable regulated work and uncertain product discovery need different risk and feedback models.

## How it works

- Waterfall is mostly sequential.
- V-model pairs development with verification.
- Incremental delivery adds usable slices.
- Iterative delivery refines repeatedly.
- Spiral delivery centres explicit risk analysis.

## Common mistakes

- Choosing by fashion.
- Calling all planned work Waterfall.
- Assuming Agile means no architecture.

## Key terms

- sequential — phases mostly in order
- incremental — usable slices over time
- iterative — repeated refinement

hu.name: SDLC Delivery Models
hu.description: Sequential, iterative, incremental és risk-driven delivery választások
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Az SDLC model eltérően rendezi a requirements, design, implementation, verification és release lépéseket.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Stable regulated work és uncertain product discovery más feedback és risk modellt igényel.

## Hogyan működik?

- Waterfall főként sequential.
- V-model development stage-et verificationnel párosít.
- Incremental delivery usable slice-okat ad.
- Iterative delivery ismételten finomít.
- Spiral explicit risk analysisra épül.

## Gyakori hibák

- Fashion alapján választani.
- Minden planned worköt Waterfallnak nevezni.
- Azt hinni, Agile mellett nincs architecture.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- sequential — phase-ek nagyrészt sorrendben
- incremental — usable slice időben
- iterative — ismételt finomítás

topics.name: User Stories and Acceptance Criteria
topics.description: A valuable need made observable and testable
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A user story starts a conversation about who needs what and why. Acceptance criteria define observable behaviour.

## What problem does it solve?

A vague ticket leaves scope, errors and success conditions open to interpretation.

## How it works

- Use INVEST as a quality check.
- Cover normal, error, permission and boundary cases.
- Use Given/When/Then where helpful.
- Split large stories by user value.

## Common mistakes

- Treating one sentence as the full requirement.
- Writing implementation steps as acceptance criteria.
- Splitting only by technical layer.

## Key terms

- INVEST — story quality checklist
- acceptance criterion — observable condition
- vertical slice — small end-to-end capability

hu.name: User Stories és Acceptance Criteria
hu.description: Valuable need observable és testable formában
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A user story conversation starter arról, kinek, mire és miért van szüksége. Az acceptance criteria observable behaviourt definiál.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A vague ticket nyitva hagyja a scope-ot, errort és success conditiont.

## Hogyan működik?

- Használd az INVEST-et quality checkként.
- Normal, error, permission és boundary case is legyen.
- Given/When/Then használható.
- Nagy storyt user value alapján splitelj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Egy mondatot teljes requirementnek tekinteni.
- Implementation stepet acceptance criteriának írni.
- Csak technical layer szerint splitelni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- INVEST — story quality checklist
- acceptance criterion — observable condition
- vertical slice — kis end-to-end capability

topics.name: Definition of Done and Ready
topics.description: Shared quality bar and optional readiness policy
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Definition of Done is the quality standard every Increment must meet. Definition of Ready is an optional policy for sufficiently understood work.

## What problem does it solve?

Without a common bar, Done can mean coded, tested, deployed or accepted depending on the person.

## How it works

- Include broad review, test, security, documentation and operational expectations.
- Keep story acceptance criteria separate.
- Improve the DoD over time.
- Use readiness to expose missing decisions, not reject all uncertainty.

## Common mistakes

- Done means development finished.
- An impossible checklist.
- Ready used as a bureaucratic gate.

## Key terms

- Definition of Done — shared Increment quality bar
- Definition of Ready — optional start policy
- hidden work — unfinished quality work

hu.name: Definition of Done és Ready
hu.description: Közös quality bar és opcionális readiness policy
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A Definition of Done minden Increment közös quality standardja. A Definition of Ready opcionális policy a sufficiently understood workhöz.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Közös bar nélkül a Done jelentheti azt, hogy coded, tested, deployed vagy accepted.

## Hogyan működik?

- Általános review, test, security, documentation és operational expectation legyen benne.
- Story acceptance criteria maradjon külön.
- A DoD idővel javuljon.
- Readiness hiányzó decisiont tárjon fel, ne minden uncertaintyt zárjon ki.

## Gyakori hibák

- Done egyenlő development finished.
- Teljesíthetetlen checklist.
- Ready mint bureaucratic gate.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- Definition of Done — közös Increment quality bar
- Definition of Ready — opcionális start policy
- hidden work — befejezetlen quality work

topics.name: Backlog Refinement and Prioritization
topics.description: Keeping upcoming work understandable and ordered
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Refinement clarifies, splits, orders and estimates upcoming work. Prioritization balances value, urgency, risk, dependency and cost.

## What problem does it solve?

An unrefined backlog turns planning into discovery and lets the loudest stakeholder control delivery.

## How it works

- Clarify outcome and success evidence.
- Surface risks and dependencies.
- Split oversized items.
- Use spikes for genuine unknowns.
- Route priority through one product decision process.

## Common mistakes

- Refining the entire long-term backlog in detail.
- Treating estimates as commitments.
- Direct stakeholder insertion into active work.

## Key terms

- refinement — preparation of upcoming work
- spike — timeboxed research
- prioritization — ordering by value and risk

hu.name: Backlog Refinement és Prioritization
hu.description: Upcoming work tisztázása, splitelése és orderelése
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A refinement tisztázza, spliteli, ordereli és estimate-eli a közelgő worköt. A prioritization value, urgency, risk, dependency és cost között egyensúlyoz.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Unrefined backlog esetén planning közben történik a discovery, és a loudest stakeholder irányít.

## Hogyan működik?

- Tisztázd az outcome-ot és success evidence-et.
- Surface-öld a risket és dependencyt.
- Spliteld az oversized itemet.
- Genuine unknownhoz spike használható.
- Priority egy product decision processen keresztül érkezzen.

## Gyakori hibák

- A teljes long-term backlog részletes refinementje.
- Estimate commitmentként kezelése.
- Direct stakeholder insertion active workbe.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- refinement — upcoming work előkészítése
- spike — timeboxed research
- prioritization — value és risk szerinti order

topics.name: Story Points and Planning Poker
topics.description: Relative estimation without pretending exact-hour certainty
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Story points estimate relative size through effort, complexity and uncertainty. Planning poker reveals estimates simultaneously and discusses differences.

## What problem does it solve?

Exact hours often imply false precision, while spoken estimates create anchoring.

## How it works

- Use reference stories and a small relative scale.
- Include uncertainty and risk.
- Reveal simultaneously.
- Discuss high and low assumptions.
- Split or spike oversized unknown work.

## Common mistakes

- Converting points to hours.
- Comparing velocity between teams.
- Velocity used as a performance KPI.

## Key terms

- story point — relative size
- planning poker — simultaneous estimation
- velocity — team planning input

hu.name: Story Points és Planning Poker
hu.description: Relative estimation exact-hour certainty látszata nélkül
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A story point effort, complexity és uncertainty alapján relative size-t becsül. A planning poker egyszerre revealeli az estimate-et, majd a különbségeket megbeszéli.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Az exact hour false precisiont, a hangosan sorban adott estimate anchoringot okoz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Reference storyt és kis scale-t használj.
- Uncertainty és risk is számítson.
- Egyszerre revealelj.
- A high és low assumption kerüljön elő.
- Oversized unknown worköt splitelj vagy spike-olj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Pointot hourrá konvertálni.
- Team velocityt összehasonlítani.
- Velocityt performance KPI-ként használni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- story point — relative size
- planning poker — simultaneous estimation
- velocity — team planning input

topics.name: Agile and Flow Metrics
topics.description: Progress and bottleneck signals rather than performance targets
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Burndown, burnup, lead time, cycle time, throughput and cumulative flow describe delivery behaviour.

## What problem does it solve?

Teams need evidence about predictability and queues, but metrics become harmful when made targets.

## How it works

- Burndown shows remaining work.
- Burnup also shows changing total scope.
- Lead time is request to delivery.
- Cycle time is started to done.
- A widening cumulative-flow band indicates a bottleneck.

## Common mistakes

- Comparing team velocity.
- Individual cycle-time targets.
- Optimizing the metric rather than the flow.

## Key terms

- lead time — customer clock
- cycle time — team clock
- Goodhart's law — targets corrupt measures

hu.name: Agile és Flow Metrics
hu.description: Progress és bottleneck signal, nem performance target
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Burndown, burnup, lead time, cycle time, throughput és cumulative flow a delivery behaviourt írja le.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Evidence kell predictabilityről és queue-ról, de a targetté tett metric káros lesz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Burndown remaining worköt mutat.
- Burnup a changing total scope-ot is megmutatja.
- Lead time requesttől deliveryig tart.
- Cycle time startedtől done-ig tart.
- Widening cumulative-flow band bottlenecket jelez.

## Gyakori hibák

- Team velocity összehasonlítása.
- Individual cycle-time target.
- Flow helyett metric optimalizálása.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- lead time — customer clock
- cycle time — team clock
- Goodhart's law — target torzítja a measure-t

topics.name: Work Breakdown and Vertical Slicing
topics.description: Small reviewable end-to-end deliverables
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Work breakdown turns a large outcome into manageable deliverables. Vertical slicing creates a thin end-to-end capability rather than isolated technical layers.

## What problem does it solve?

Large tickets delay feedback, increase review risk and hide progress.

## How it works

- Start from a user or business outcome.
- Pull major risk forward.
- Create narrow end-to-end slices.
- Include tests, observability, rollout and rollback.
- Keep changes reviewable and deployable where possible.

## Common mistakes

- One ticket for the entire feature.
- Frontend/backend/database stories with no standalone value.
- Ignoring rollout work.

## Key terms

- deliverable — concrete verifiable result
- vertical slice — thin end-to-end capability
- work breakdown — decomposition into units

hu.name: Work Breakdown és Vertical Slicing
hu.description: Kis reviewable end-to-end deliverable-ök
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A work breakdown nagy outcome-ot manageable deliverable-ökre bont. A vertical slicing vékony end-to-end capabilityt készít isolated technical layer helyett.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A nagy ticket késlelteti a feedbacket, növeli a review risket és elrejti a progresst.

## Hogyan működik?

- User vagy business outcome-ból indulj.
- A major risket hozd előre.
- Narrow end-to-end slice-ot készíts.
- Test, observability, rollout és rollback is legyen benne.
- A change lehetőleg reviewable és deployable legyen.

## Gyakori hibák

- Egy ticketbe tenni az egész feature-t.
- Standalone value nélküli frontend/backend/database story.
- Rollout work kihagyása.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- deliverable — konkrét verifikálható result
- vertical slice — vékony end-to-end capability
- work breakdown — unitokra bontás

topics.name: Extreme Programming Practices
topics.description: TDD, pairing, continuous integration and refactoring
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

XP focuses on engineering practices that allow software to change safely and frequently.

## What problem does it solve?

Scrum ceremonies alone do not stop a codebase from becoming harder to change every Sprint.

## How it works

- Use TDD where fast behavioural feedback helps.
- Pair on risky or knowledge-heavy work.
- Integrate small changes frequently.
- Refactor continuously.
- Prefer simple design for current needs.

## Common mistakes

- Treating XP as only TDD.
- Applying practices as rituals.
- Deferring all refactoring to a future Sprint.

## Key terms

- TDD — red, green, refactor
- pair programming — driver and navigator
- continuous integration — frequent verified merge

hu.name: Extreme Programming Practices
hu.description: TDD, pairing, continuous integration és refactoring
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Az XP engineering practice-ekre fókuszál, amelyekkel a software safe és gyakran változtatható.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A Scrum ceremony önmagában nem akadályozza meg, hogy a codebase minden Sprintben nehezebben változzon.

## Hogyan működik?

- TDD-t használj, ahol fast behavioural feedback segít.
- Risky vagy knowledge-heavy worknél pair-elj.
- Small change-et gyakran integrálj.
- Folyamatosan refactorálj.
- Current needhez simple designt válassz.

## Gyakori hibák

- Az XP-t csak TDD-nek tekinteni.
- Ritualként alkalmazni.
- Minden refactoringot későbbi Sprintre tolni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- TDD — red, green, refactor
- pair programming — driver és navigator
- continuous integration — frequent verified merge

topics.name: Test-Driven Development
topics.description: Red-Green-Refactor in small verified steps
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

TDD writes a small failing test, adds the minimum passing implementation and then improves design while tests stay green.

## What problem does it solve?

It creates fast behavioural feedback and a regression safety net.

## How it works

- Test one observable behaviour.
- Confirm failure for the expected reason.
- Add minimal code.
- Refactor without behaviour change.
- Use integration tests when risk is at a boundary.

## Common mistakes

- Testing private implementation details.
- Treating TDD as mandatory for every exploratory task.
- Skipping refactoring.

## Key terms

- red — expected failing test
- green — minimum passing code
- refactor — improve structure safely

hu.name: Test-Driven Development
hu.description: Red-Green-Refactor kis verified step-ekben
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A TDD kis failing testet ír, minimal passing implementationt ad, majd green test mellett javítja a designt.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Fast behavioural feedbacket és regression safety netet ad.

## Hogyan működik?

- Egy observable behaviourt tesztelj.
- A test a várt okból failoljon.
- Minimal code kerüljön be.
- Behaviour change nélkül refactorálj.
- Boundary riskhez integration test kell.

## Gyakori hibák

- Private implementation detailt tesztelni.
- Minden exploratory taskhoz kötelezővé tenni.
- Refactoring kihagyása.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- red — expected failing test
- green — minimum passing code
- refactor — safe structure improvement

topics.name: Testing Strategy
topics.description: Matching risks to unit, integration, contract and E2E tests
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A testing strategy assigns risks to appropriate layers: unit, integration, contract and a small number of end-to-end tests.

## What problem does it solve?

One test type cannot provide fast feedback, realistic boundaries and broad confidence at equal cost.

## How it works

- Unit test deterministic logic.
- Integration test real boundaries.
- Contract test independently deployed interfaces.
- E2E test only critical journeys.
- Track flakiness and feedback time.

## Common mistakes

- Testing implementation details.
- Replacing all tests with E2E.
- Mocking every real boundary.
- Ignoring flaky tests.

## Key terms

- unit test — isolated logic
- integration test — real components together
- contract test — interface agreement
- E2E — complete journey

hu.name: Testing Strategy
hu.description: Risk hozzárendelése unit, integration, contract és E2E testhez
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A testing strategy a riskeket megfelelő layerhez rendeli: unit, integration, contract és kevés end-to-end test.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Egy test type nem ad azonos cost mellett fast feedbacket, realistic boundaryt és broad confidence-t.

## Hogyan működik?

- Deterministic logichoz unit test.
- Real boundaryhoz integration test.
- Independently deployed interface-hez contract test.
- E2E csak critical journeyre.
- Flakiness és feedback time legyen követve.

## Gyakori hibák

- Implementation detailt tesztelni.
- Minden testet E2E-re cserélni.
- Minden real boundaryt mockolni.
- Flaky testet ignorálni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- unit test — isolated logic
- integration test — real componentek együtt
- contract test — interface agreement
- E2E — complete journey

topics.name: Code Review and Pair Programming
topics.description: Collaborative quality and knowledge sharing
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Code review evaluates a proposed change. Pair programming has two engineers collaborate continuously, commonly as driver and navigator.

## What problem does it solve?

Both expose assumptions, catch defects and reduce single-person ownership of critical code.

## How it works

- Keep changes small.
- Review correctness, tests, security, operations and maintainability.
- Distinguish blocking issues from suggestions.
- Pair on complex, risky or knowledge-transfer work.

## Common mistakes

- Review only for style.
- Giant pull requests.
- Comments aimed at the author.
- Passive pairing.

## Key terms

- blocking comment — must resolve before merge
- driver — controls keyboard
- navigator — reviews and thinks ahead

hu.name: Code Review és Pair Programming
hu.description: Collaborative quality és knowledge sharing
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A code review proposed change-et vizsgál. Pair programmingnél két engineer folyamatosan együtt dolgozik driver és navigator szerepben.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Mindkettő hidden assumptiont és defectet tár fel, valamint csökkenti a critical code single-person ownershipját.

## Hogyan működik?

- A change legyen small.
- Correctness, test, security, operations és maintainability legyen reviewzva.
- Blocking issue és suggestion különüljön el.
- Complex, risky vagy knowledge-transfer worknél pair-elj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Review csak style miatt.
- Giant pull request.
- Authorra irányuló comment.
- Passive pairing.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- blocking comment — merge előtt megoldandó
- driver — keyboardot kezel
- navigator — reviewz és előre gondolkodik

topics.name: Continuous Integration and Branching
topics.description: Frequent verified integration with short-lived branches
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Continuous integration means merging small changes frequently into a shared mainline with automated verification.

## What problem does it solve?

Long-lived branches accumulate incompatible assumptions and turn integration into a risky final project.

## How it works

- Merge small changes frequently.
- Run fast checks on every integration.
- Keep main releasable.
- Use flags or compatibility for incomplete work.
- Fix broken main immediately.

## Common mistakes

- Weekly merging called CI.
- Months-long feature branches.
- Feature flags without owners or removal.

## Key terms

- mainline — shared integration branch
- trunk-based — frequent mainline integration
- integration debt — delayed merge cost

hu.name: Continuous Integration és Branching
hu.description: Frequent verified integration short-lived branch mellett
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Continuous integration esetén small change gyakran kerül shared mainline-ba automated verification mellett.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A long-lived branch incompatible assumptiont halmoz, az integration pedig risky final project lesz.

## Hogyan működik?

- Small change-et gyakran merge-elj.
- Minden integrationnél fast check fusson.
- Main maradjon releasable.
- Incomplete workhöz flag vagy compatibility használható.
- Broken maint azonnal javíts.

## Gyakori hibák

- Heti merge-et CI-nek nevezni.
- Több hónapos feature branch.
- Owner és removal nélküli feature flag.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- mainline — shared integration branch
- trunk-based — frequent mainline integration
- integration debt — delayed merge cost

topics.name: Technical Debt and Refactoring
topics.description: Managing future change cost and risk
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Technical debt is future cost or risk created by a compromise. Refactoring improves internal design without observable behaviour change.

## What problem does it solve?

Invisible debt slows delivery and increases defects, but calling every disliked design debt destroys prioritization.

## How it works

- Describe business impact.
- Record affected areas and triggers.
- Fix small debt continuously.
- Create explicit work for larger risk.
- Measure improvement.

## Common mistakes

- Giant rewrite called debt removal.
- Refactoring stable code for style.
- Vague debt with no impact.

## Key terms

- debt interest — recurring cost
- refactoring — internal improvement without behaviour change
- rewrite — replacement

hu.name: Technical Debt és Refactoring
hu.description: Future change cost és risk kezelése
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A technical debt compromise által létrehozott future cost vagy risk. A refactoring observable behaviour change nélkül javítja az internal designt.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Invisible debt lassít és defectet növel, de ha minden nem kedvelt design debt, nincs prioritization.

## Hogyan működik?

- Business impacttal írd le.
- Affected area és trigger legyen rögzítve.
- Kis debtet folyamatosan javíts.
- Nagy riskhez explicit work kell.
- Mérd az improvementet.

## Gyakori hibák

- Giant rewrite debt removal néven.
- Stable code refactor style miatt.
- Impact nélküli vague debt.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- debt interest — recurring cost
- refactoring — internal improvement behaviour change nélkül
- rewrite — replacement

topics.name: Retrospectives and Continuous Improvement
topics.description: Small owned improvements based on evidence
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A retrospective inspects how the team worked and selects changes. Continuous improvement repeatedly adapts the system of work.

## What problem does it solve?

Discussion without ownership and follow-up repeats the same problems.

## How it works

- Use evidence such as delays, incidents and flow data.
- Select one or two actions.
- Assign owner and review date.
- Treat changes as experiments.
- Review previous actions first.

## Common mistakes

- Long action lists.
- No owners.
- Management evaluation.
- Skipping retros when busy.

## Key terms

- retrospective — inspection of working method
- action item — owned improvement
- experiment — change with expected signal

hu.name: Retrospectives és Continuous Improvement
hu.description: Evidence alapú small owned improvement
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A retrospective inspectálja a team working methodját és change-et választ. Continuous improvement rendszeresen adaptálja a working systemet.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Ownership és follow-up nélküli discussion ugyanazt a problémát ismétli.

## Hogyan működik?

- Delay, incident és flow data evidence-et használj.
- Egy-két actiont válassz.
- Legyen owner és review date.
- Change-et experimentként kezeld.
- Először az előző actiont reviewzd.

## Gyakori hibák

- Long action list.
- Owner hiánya.
- Management evaluation.
- Busy időszakban retros kihagyása.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- retrospective — working method inspection
- action item — owned improvement
- experiment — expected signallal rendelkező change

topics.name: Incident Postmortems
topics.description: Blameless learning with timelines and owned prevention
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

A postmortem reconstructs impact, timeline, contributing factors, response and corrective actions.

## What problem does it solve?

Stopping after recovery leaves the same conditions in place; blaming one person hides system causes.

## How it works

- Record user and business impact.
- Build a factual timeline.
- Identify contributing and detection gaps.
- Explain decisions in their original context.
- Create specific owned actions.

## Common mistakes

- Human error as root cause.
- Vague be-careful action.
- Long report with no prevention.

## Key terms

- contributing factor — condition increasing risk
- blameless — system learning instead of punishment
- corrective action — owned prevention

hu.name: Incident Postmortems
hu.description: Blameless learning timeline-nal és owned preventionnel
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A postmortem rekonstruálja az impactet, timeline-t, contributing factort, response-t és corrective actiont.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Recovery után megállva ugyanazok a conditionök maradnak; egy ember hibáztatása elrejti a system cause-okat.

## Hogyan működik?

- Rögzíts user és business impactet.
- Készíts factual timeline-t.
- Keresd a contributing és detection gapet.
- A decisiont eredeti contextjében értékeld.
- Specific owned action készüljön.

## Gyakori hibák

- Human error mint root cause.
- Vague be-careful action.
- Prevention nélküli hosszú report.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- contributing factor — risket növelő condition
- blameless — punishment helyett system learning
- corrective action — owned prevention

topics.name: Technical Leadership and Mentoring
topics.description: Direction and team enablement without micromanagement
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Technical leadership clarifies requirements, compares options, names risks, documents decisions, reviews changes, mentors and unblocks delivery.

## What problem does it solve?

A team needs coherent direction without one person becoming the decision bottleneck.

## How it works

- Make goals, constraints and trade-offs visible.
- Involve the team in decisions.
- Delegate bounded ownership.
- Create review points.
- Coach through questions, pairing and examples.

## Common mistakes

- Making every decision alone.
- Micromanaging implementation.
- Abstract architecture without execution plan.

## Key terms

- enablement — increasing team independence
- delegation — ownership with boundaries
- mentoring — longer-term skill growth

hu.name: Technical Leadership és Mentoring
hu.description: Direction és team enablement micromanagement nélkül
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A technical leadership requirementet tisztáz, optiont hasonlít, risket nevez meg, decisiont dokumentál, reviewz, mentorál és unblockol.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A teamnek coherent direction kell úgy, hogy egy ember ne legyen decision bottleneck.

## Hogyan működik?

- Goal, constraint és trade-off legyen látható.
- A team legyen bevonva a decisionbe.
- Bounded ownershipot delegálj.
- Review pointot hozz létre.
- Question, pairing és example segítségével coacholj.

## Gyakori hibák

- Minden decisiont egyedül meghozni.
- Implementationt micromanagelni.
- Execution plan nélküli abstract architecture.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- enablement — team independence növelése
- delegation — ownership boundaryvel
- mentoring — hosszabb távú skill growth

topics.name: Stakeholder and Product Collaboration
topics.description: Turning business goals into technical decisions and explicit trade-offs
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

Stakeholder collaboration connects customer outcomes with technical design and delivery.

## What problem does it solve?

Unfiltered requests create conflicting priorities, while technical risks remain hidden if engineers communicate only implementation detail.

## How it works

- Ask for the desired outcome and success measure.
- Clarify deadline, scope flexibility and constraints.
- Express technical risk as business impact and options.
- Route changes through product prioritization.
- Use demos for early feedback.

## Common mistakes

- Saying no with jargon.
- Accepting every request directly into active work.
- Hiding uncertainty until late.

## Key terms

- product outcome — measurable user or business result
- scope flexibility — what may change
- decision record — choice and alternatives

hu.name: Stakeholder és Product Collaboration
hu.description: Business goal technikai decisionné és explicit trade-offá alakítása
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

A stakeholder collaboration customer outcome-ot köt technical designhoz és deliveryhez.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

Unfiltered request conflicting priorityt okoz; technical risk rejtve marad, ha csak implementation detailről beszélünk.

## Hogyan működik?

- Kérdezd meg a desired outcome-ot és success measure-t.
- Tisztázd a deadline-t, scope flexibilityt és constraintet.
- Technical risket business impact és option formájában kommunikálj.
- Change product prioritizationön keresztül érkezzen.
- Demo adjon early feedbacket.

## Gyakori hibák

- Jargonnal nemet mondani.
- Minden requestet active workbe tenni.
- Uncertaintyt későig rejteni.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- product outcome — measurable user vagy business result
- scope flexibility — változtatható rész
- decision record — choice és alternative

topics.name: AI-Assisted Development
topics.description: AI as an accelerator with human review and ownership
topics.studyNotes:
## What is it?

AI tools can generate code, tests, documentation and options, while the engineer remains responsible for the result.

## What problem does it solve?

Generated output may be plausible but wrong, insecure or inconsistent; faster generation increases verification needs.

## How it works

- Use AI for bounded repetitive work and exploration.
- Provide constraints and architecture context.
- Review correctness, security, performance and maintainability.
- Apply tests, types and normal review.
- Never ship code the team cannot explain.

## Common mistakes

- Generated output treated as reviewed.
- Unverified APIs or dependencies.
- Architecture ownership outsourced to the tool.

## Key terms

- guardrail — tests, types, review or policy
- explainability — ability to understand and maintain
- ownership — human responsibility

hu.name: AI-Assisted Development
hu.description: AI mint accelerator human review és ownership mellett
hu.studyNotes:
## Mi ez?

Az AI code-ot, testet, documentationt és optiont generálhat, de a resultért továbbra is az engineer felel.

## Milyen problémát old meg?

A generated output lehet plausible, mégis wrong, insecure vagy inconsistent; a gyors generation erősebb verificationt igényel.

## Hogyan működik?

- Bounded repetitive workre és explorationre használd.
- Adj constraintet és architecture contextet.
- Reviewzd a correctness-t, securityt, performance-et és maintainabilityt.
- Test, type és normál review szükséges.
- Ne shipelj olyan code-ot, amelyet a team nem ért.

## Gyakori hibák

- Generated output reviewedként kezelése.
- Unverified API vagy dependency.
- Architecture ownership kiszervezése a toolnak.

## Kulcsfogalmak

- guardrail — test, type, review vagy policy
- explainability — megérthetőség és maintainability
- ownership — human responsibility

questions.title: Choosing Scrum or Kanban
questions.prompt: A product team develops planned features, while a support team receives unpredictable requests. Which workflow would you use and why?
questions.answerStructureHint: Compare cadence, incoming-work pattern, WIP, goals and feedback instead of declaring one method universally better.

expectedPoints.label: Uses Scrum where a Sprint Goal helps
expectedPoints.description: Chooses Scrum for planned product work benefiting from a short goal and review cadence.

expectedPoints.label: Uses Kanban for continuous flow
expectedPoints.description: Chooses Kanban for unpredictable work with WIP limits and pull policies.

expectedPoints.label: Uses context rather than dogma
expectedPoints.description: Considers arrival pattern, risk, team and feedback needs.

expectedPoints.label: Names suitable signals
expectedPoints.description: Uses Sprint Goal or forecast signals for Scrum and flow metrics for Kanban.

followUps.prompt: Could one team combine Scrum cadence with Kanban-style WIP limits?

hu.title: Scrum vagy Kanban választása
hu.prompt: Egy product team planned feature-öket fejleszt, egy support team pedig unpredictable requesteket kap. Mely workflow-t használnád, és miért?
hu.answerStructureHint: Cadence, incoming-work pattern, WIP, goal és feedback alapján hasonlíts.

ask_scrum.label: Scrumot választ, ahol Sprint Goal segít
ask_scrum.description: Planned product worknél short goal és review cadence indokolt.

ask_kanban.label: Kanbant választ continuous flow-hoz
ask_kanban.description: Unpredictable worknél WIP limit és pull policy szerepel.

ask_context.label: Context alapján dönt
ask_context.description: Arrival pattern, risk, team és feedback need alapján választ.

ask_metrics.label: Megfelelő signalt nevez meg
ask_metrics.description: Scrumnál Sprint Goal vagy forecast, Kanbannál flow metric szerepel.

followUps.ask_follow_mix: Kombinálhatja-e egy team a Scrum cadence-t Kanban-style WIP limittel?

questions.title: Refining an unclear story
questions.prompt: The backlog contains Improve account security with no acceptance criteria. How would you refine it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Clarify outcome, scope and risks; create observable criteria; split valuable work; separate story criteria from the general quality bar.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies the outcome
expectedPoints.description: Identifies user, problem, desired result and success evidence.

expectedPoints.label: Clarifies constraints
expectedPoints.description: Surfaces permissions, compliance, migration, threats and operations as relevant.

expectedPoints.label: Creates observable criteria
expectedPoints.description: Covers normal, error and boundary behaviour rather than implementation steps.

expectedPoints.label: Splits valuable work
expectedPoints.description: Creates small end-to-end deliverables or a spike for genuine unknowns.

followUps.prompt: Which requirements belong in the story and which belong in the Definition of Done?

hu.title: Egy tisztázatlan story refinementje
hu.prompt: A backlogban csak Improve account security szerepel acceptance criteria nélkül. Hogyan refinementelnéd?
hu.answerStructureHint: Outcome, scope és risk, observable criteria, valuable split és story criteria vs quality bar.

ars_outcome.label: Tisztázza az outcome-ot
ars_outcome.description: User, problem, desired result és success evidence szerepel.

ars_scope.label: Tisztázza a constraintet
ars_scope.description: Permission, compliance, migration, threat és operations szükség szerint előkerül.

ars_criteria.label: Observable criteriát készít
ars_criteria.description: Normal, error és boundary behaviour szerepel implementation step helyett.

ars_split.label: Valuable workre bont
ars_split.description: Small end-to-end deliverable vagy genuine unknown esetén spike készül.

followUps.ars_follow_done: Mi tartozik a storyba, és mi a Definition of Done-ba?

questions.title: Velocity used as a performance KPI
questions.prompt: Management compares team velocities and demands a 20 percent point increase. What will happen, and what would you use instead?
questions.answerStructureHint: Explain relative team-local points, metric gaming, correct planning use and better outcome or flow signals.

expectedPoints.label: Explains team-relative points
expectedPoints.description: States that points are not comparable across teams or fixed hours.

expectedPoints.label: Predicts gaming
expectedPoints.description: Explains point inflation and behaviour distortion when velocity becomes a target.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps velocity for planning
expectedPoints.description: Uses recent velocity only for one team's near-term forecast.

expectedPoints.label: Suggests better signals
expectedPoints.description: Uses product outcomes, lead or cycle time, quality or customer impact with context.

followUps.prompt: How would you explain this to an executive who still wants one simple number?

hu.title: Velocity mint performance KPI
hu.prompt: A management team velocitykat hasonlít össze és 20 százalékos pointnövekedést kér. Mi történik, és mit használnál helyette?
hu.answerStructureHint: Team-relative point, gaming, correct planning use és jobb outcome vagy flow signal.

avk_relative.label: Elmagyarázza a team-relative pointot
avk_relative.description: A point team-ek között nem összehasonlítható és nem fixed hour.

avk_gaming.label: Felismeri a gaminget
avk_gaming.description: Velocity target point inflationt és behaviour distortiont okoz.

avk_planning.label: Planningre tartja meg
avk_planning.description: Recent velocity csak egy team near-term forecastját segíti.

avk_outcomes.label: Jobb signalt javasol
avk_outcomes.description: Product outcome, lead vagy cycle time, quality vagy customer impact szerepel.

followUps.avk_follow_exec: Hogyan magyaráznád ezt egy executive-nak, aki egyetlen egyszerű számot akar?

questions.title: Breaking down a large platform feature
questions.prompt: An integration feature includes API contract, database migration, mapping, authentication, tests, monitoring and rollout. How would you split it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Start from a usable outcome, create thin end-to-end slices, pull risk forward and include operational completion.

expectedPoints.label: Starts with a usable outcome
expectedPoints.description: Finds the smallest demonstrable customer or operational capability.

expectedPoints.label: Creates vertical slices
expectedPoints.description: Combines enough layers for meaningful validation instead of layer-only tickets.

expectedPoints.label: Pulls risk forward
expectedPoints.description: Uses contract-first work, spikes or compatibility steps to test assumptions early.

expectedPoints.label: Includes operational work
expectedPoints.description: Includes tests, observability, migration, rollout and rollback.

followUps.prompt: Where could a feature flag help, and where would it not solve the migration problem?

hu.title: Egy nagy platform feature lebontása
hu.prompt: Egy integration feature API contractot, database migrationt, mappinget, authenticationt, testet, monitoringot és rolloutot tartalmaz. Hogyan splitelnéd?
hu.answerStructureHint: Usable outcome, thin end-to-end slice, early risk és operational completion.

asf_outcome.label: Usable outcome-ból indul
asf_outcome.description: A legkisebb demonstrálható customer vagy operational capabilityt keresi.

asf_vertical.label: Vertical slice-ot készít
asf_vertical.description: Meaningful validationt ad technical-layer ticket helyett.

asf_risk.label: Előrehozza a risket
asf_risk.description: Contract-first work, spike vagy compatibility step teszteli az assumptiont.

asf_ops.label: Operational worköt is beépít
asf_ops.description: Test, observability, migration, rollout és rollback része a worknek.

followUps.asf_follow_flag: Hol segíthet feature flag, és hol nem oldja meg a migration problémát?

questions.title: When TDD helps and when it does not
questions.prompt: How would you use TDD for a pricing-rule engine, and why might you not use the same approach for a short visual prototype?
questions.answerStructureHint: Describe Red-Green-Refactor for deterministic rules and compare feedback value with exploratory work.

expectedPoints.label: Explains Red-Green-Refactor
expectedPoints.description: Uses a failing test, minimal passing code and safe refactoring.

expectedPoints.label: Matches TDD to rule logic
expectedPoints.description: Uses it where examples, edge cases and regression risk make behavioural feedback valuable.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids dogma
expectedPoints.description: Explains why exploratory visual work may need learning first and different tests later.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses boundary tests
expectedPoints.description: Uses integration or contract tests when risk is at an external boundary.

followUps.prompt: How would you introduce TDD around untested legacy code?

hu.title: Mikor segít a TDD, és mikor nem
hu.prompt: Hogyan használnál TDD-t pricing-rule engine esetén, és miért nem feltétlenül ugyanígy egy rövid visual prototype-nál?
hu.answerStructureHint: Red-Green-Refactor deterministic rule-höz, majd feedback value exploratory worknél.

atc_cycle.label: Elmagyarázza a Red-Green-Refactor cycle-t
atc_cycle.description: Failing test, minimal passing code és safe refactoring szerepel.

atc_fit.label: Rule logichoz illeszti
atc_fit.description: Example, edge case és regression risk esetén értékes.

atc_nodogma.label: Nem dogmatikusan használja
atc_nodogma.description: Exploratory visual worknél learning first és későbbi más test lehet jobb.

atc_layers.label: Boundary testet választ
atc_layers.description: External boundary riskhez integration vagy contract test kerül.

followUps.atc_follow_legacy: Hogyan vezetnéd be a TDD-t untested legacy code körül?

questions.title: Designing a test strategy
questions.prompt: Design a test strategy for a service with business rules, a database, a public API and three critical user journeys.
questions.answerStructureHint: Map risks to unit, integration, contract and focused E2E layers.

expectedPoints.label: Uses unit tests for rules
expectedPoints.description: Tests deterministic logic and edge cases cheaply.

expectedPoints.label: Tests real boundaries
expectedPoints.description: Covers database, framework and API serialization behaviour.

expectedPoints.label: Protects the contract
expectedPoints.description: Uses contract or schema tests for public API compatibility.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps focused E2E coverage
expectedPoints.description: Automates a few critical journeys without making the whole suite slow E2E.

followUps.prompt: What would you do when the E2E suite becomes flaky and teams stop trusting it?

hu.title: Testing strategy tervezése
hu.prompt: Tervezz testing strategyt business rule-t, database-t, public API-t és három critical user journeyt tartalmazó service-hez.
hu.answerStructureHint: Risket rendelj unit, integration, contract és focused E2E layerhez.

ats_unit.label: Unit testet használ rule-höz
ats_unit.description: Deterministic logic és edge case olcsón tesztelt.

ats_integration.label: Real boundaryt tesztel
ats_integration.description: Database, framework és API serialization behaviour szerepel.

ats_contract.label: Védi a contractot
ats_contract.description: Public API compatibility contract vagy schema testtel védett.

ats_e2e.label: Focused E2E coverage-et tart
ats_e2e.description: Kevés critical journey automated, a teljes suite nem slow E2E.

followUps.ats_follow_flaky: Mit tennél, ha az E2E suite flaky lenne, és a team nem bízna benne?

questions.title: A pull request is too large
questions.prompt: A developer opens a 6,000-line pull request after three weeks. How would you review it and prevent recurrence?
questions.answerStructureHint: Reduce immediate review risk, keep collaboration constructive, then change slicing and integration habits.

expectedPoints.label: Manages current risk
expectedPoints.description: Separates mechanical and behavioural changes and requests smaller reviewable segments where possible.

expectedPoints.label: Prioritizes important review areas
expectedPoints.description: Checks correctness, migrations, security, tests, rollout and operations.

expectedPoints.label: Keeps review constructive
expectedPoints.description: Uses a walkthrough or pairing and comments on decisions rather than the author.

expectedPoints.label: Changes the work system
expectedPoints.description: Introduces smaller slices, short-lived branches, early review and frequent integration.

followUps.prompt: Does reviewer approval transfer responsibility away from the author?

hu.title: Túl nagy pull request
hu.prompt: Egy developer három hét után 6000 soros pull requestet nyit. Hogyan reviewznád és előznéd meg az ismétlődést?
hu.answerStructureHint: Immediate review risk, constructive collaboration, majd slicing és integration habit.

alp_current.label: Kezeli a current risket
alp_current.description: Mechanical és behavioural change-et különít, és ahol lehet kisebb reviewable részt kér.

alp_quality.label: Fontos review területre fókuszál
alp_quality.description: Correctness, migration, security, test, rollout és operations szerepel.

alp_collab.label: Constructive marad
alp_collab.description: Walkthrough vagy pairing történik, a comment a decisionre irányul.

alp_prevent.label: Megváltoztatja a working systemet
alp_prevent.description: Smaller slice, short-lived branch, early review és frequent integration kerül be.

followUps.alp_follow_resp: A reviewer approval átadja-e a responsibilityt az authorról?

questions.title: Prioritizing technical debt
questions.prompt: The team requests a six-month rewrite while product wants features. How would you evaluate the debt?
questions.answerStructureHint: Replace the vague rewrite with concrete impact, compare options and create incremental measurable work.

expectedPoints.label: Makes debt concrete
expectedPoints.description: Connects affected areas to delay, defects, incidents, security or operating cost.

expectedPoints.label: Compares alternatives
expectedPoints.description: Considers targeted refactoring, strangler replacement, testing or no change.

expectedPoints.label: Creates incremental work
expectedPoints.description: Uses small owned backlog items or nearby refactoring.

expectedPoints.label: Defines evidence
expectedPoints.description: Measures lead time, defects, incidents or risk after changes.

followUps.prompt: What evidence could justify a full rewrite?

hu.title: Technical debt priorizálása
hu.prompt: A team hat hónapos rewrite-ot kér, a product pedig feature-öket akar. Hogyan értékelnéd a debtet?
hu.answerStructureHint: Vague rewrite helyett concrete impact, option comparison és incremental measurable work.

adr_impact.label: Concrete-tá teszi a debtet
adr_impact.description: Delay, defect, incident, security vagy operating cost kapcsolat szerepel.

adr_options.label: Alternative-okat hasonlít
adr_options.description: Targeted refactoring, strangler, testing vagy no change kerül elő.

adr_slice.label: Incremental worköt készít
adr_slice.description: Small owned backlog item vagy nearby refactoring történik.

adr_measure.label: Evidence-et definiál
adr_measure.description: Lead time, defect, incident vagy risk változása mérhető.

questions.title: Retrospectives produce no change
questions.prompt: The same issues appear in every retrospective and action items are forgotten. How would you fix the process?
questions.answerStructureHint: Use evidence, select few actions, assign ownership and review experiments.

expectedPoints.label: Uses evidence
expectedPoints.description: Brings delays, rework, incidents or flow data.

expectedPoints.label: Selects few actions
expectedPoints.description: Chooses one or two high-value improvements.

expectedPoints.label: Adds ownership
expectedPoints.description: Assigns an owner and review date and checks previous actions first.

expectedPoints.label: Uses experiments
expectedPoints.description: Defines expected results and later keeps, changes or stops the intervention.

followUps.prompt: How would you respond if people fear blame and do not speak honestly?

hu.title: A retrospective nem eredményez változást
hu.prompt: Ugyanazok a problémák jelennek meg minden retrospective-en, az action itemeket pedig elfelejtik. Hogyan javítanád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Evidence, kevés action, ownership és experiment review.

ara_evidence.label: Evidence-et használ
ara_evidence.description: Delay, rework, incident vagy flow data kerül elő.

ara_owner.label: Ownershipot ad
ara_owner.description: Owner és review date van, az előző action először reviewzott.

ara_experiment.label: Experimentet használ
ara_experiment.description: Expected result alapján később keep, change vagy stop decision történik.

followUps.ara_follow_safety: Mit tennél, ha az emberek blame-től félve nem beszélnének őszintén?

questions.title: A postmortem blames human error
questions.prompt: An outage report concludes that an engineer deployed the wrong configuration and says be more careful. How would you improve it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Build a factual timeline, identify system contributors and detection gaps, then create specific owned controls.

expectedPoints.label: Builds a timeline
expectedPoints.description: Uses logs, deployment records, alerts and communication history.

expectedPoints.label: Finds contributing factors
expectedPoints.description: Examines defaults, access, review, tooling and available information.

expectedPoints.label: Finds detection gaps
expectedPoints.description: Checks why the configuration was not prevented or detected earlier.

expectedPoints.label: Creates specific actions
expectedPoints.description: Uses automation, validation, safer rollout or access controls with owners.

followUps.prompt: How can a postmortem be blameless while people remain accountable?

hu.title: Egy postmortem human errort hibáztat
hu.prompt: Egy outage report szerint egy engineer rossz configurationt deployolt, és az action be more careful. Hogyan javítanád?
hu.answerStructureHint: Factual timeline, system contributor, detection gap és specific owned control.

apm_timeline.label: Timeline-t készít
apm_timeline.description: Log, deployment record, alert és communication history alapján dolgozik.

apm_context.label: Contributing factort keres
apm_context.description: Default, access, review, tooling és available information előkerül.

apm_gaps.label: Detection gapet keres
apm_gaps.description: Megvizsgálja, miért nem volt prevent vagy earlier detection.

apm_actions.label: Specific actiont készít
apm_actions.description: Automation, validation, safer rollout vagy access control kap ownert.

followUps.apm_follow_account: Hogyan lehet blameless a postmortem úgy, hogy accountability is marad?

questions.title: Leading without becoming the bottleneck
questions.prompt: You lead a feature involving several developers and teams. How do you provide direction without deciding everything yourself?
questions.answerStructureHint: Provide shared context, delegate bounded ownership, create review points and coach.

expectedPoints.label: Provides shared context
expectedPoints.description: Clarifies goals, constraints, NFRs, risks and decision principles.

expectedPoints.label: Delegates bounded ownership
expectedPoints.description: Gives clear responsibility and freedom inside agreed boundaries.

expectedPoints.label: Creates review points
expectedPoints.description: Uses design review, code review, demos and risk checkpoints.

expectedPoints.label: Coaches and documents
expectedPoints.description: Uses questions, pairing, examples and decision records.

followUps.prompt: What would you do when a delegated design is moving in an unsafe direction?

hu.title: Leadership bottleneck nélkül
hu.prompt: Több developert és teamet érintő feature-t vezetsz. Hogyan adsz directiont úgy, hogy ne mindent te dönts el?
hu.answerStructureHint: Shared context, bounded ownership, review point és coaching.

alb_context.label: Shared contextet ad
alb_context.description: Goal, constraint, NFR, risk és decision principle tisztázott.

alb_delegate.label: Bounded ownershipot delegál
alb_delegate.description: Clear responsibility és decision freedom van agreed boundaryn belül.

alb_review.label: Review pointot készít
alb_review.description: Design review, code review, demo és risk checkpoint szerepel.

alb_coach.label: Coachol és dokumentál
alb_coach.description: Question, pairing, example és decision record növeli a capabilityt.

followUps.alb_follow_unsafe: Mit tennél, ha egy delegated design unsafe irányba haladna?

questions.title: Stakeholders change requirements during a Sprint
questions.prompt: A stakeholder repeatedly asks developers directly to add urgent work to the current Sprint. How would you handle it?
questions.answerStructureHint: Protect the goal and decision process while handling genuine emergencies explicitly.

expectedPoints.label: Protects the Sprint Goal
expectedPoints.description: Evaluates impact on the current objective rather than treating the plan as frozen or disposable.

expectedPoints.label: Uses the priority owner
expectedPoints.description: Routes the request through Product Owner or agreed decision owner.

expectedPoints.label: Makes trade-offs visible
expectedPoints.description: Explains what scope, risk, quality or date changes if the work enters.

expectedPoints.label: Handles real emergencies
expectedPoints.description: Uses an agreed expedite or incident process for security, outage or legal emergencies.

followUps.prompt: When would cancelling a Sprint be justified?

hu.title: Stakeholder Sprint közben változtat requirementet
hu.prompt: Egy stakeholder rendszeresen közvetlenül a developerektől kér urgent worköt a current Sprintbe. Hogyan kezelnéd?
hu.answerStructureHint: Sprint Goal és decision process védelme, genuine emergency explicit kezelése.

acs_goal.label: Védi a Sprint Goalt
acs_goal.description: A current objective-re gyakorolt impactet vizsgálja; a plan nem frozen és nem disposable.

acs_route.label: Priority owneren keresztül kezeli
acs_route.description: A request Product Ownerhez vagy agreed decision ownerhez kerül.

acs_trade.label: Láthatóvá teszi a trade-offot
acs_trade.description: Scope, risk, quality vagy date változása explicit.

acs_emergency.label: Real emergencyt kezel
acs_emergency.description: Security, outage vagy legal emergency agreed expedite vagy incident processt kap.

followUps.acs_follow_cancel: Mikor lenne indokolt egy Sprint cancelölése?

questions.title: Choosing an SDLC model
questions.prompt: Compare delivery approaches for a regulated system with stable requirements and an experimental product with uncertain requirements.
questions.answerStructureHint: Match requirement volatility, compliance, verification, risk and feedback cost to the delivery model.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses controlled verification for regulated work
expectedPoints.description: Explains why sequential planning or a V-model may help traceability and verification when change is expensive.

expectedPoints.label: Chooses iterative learning for uncertainty
expectedPoints.description: Uses incremental or iterative delivery to test uncertain product assumptions early.

expectedPoints.label: Uses risk as a decision factor
expectedPoints.description: Considers spiral-style explicit risk work for large high-risk unknowns.

expectedPoints.label: Avoids false binaries
expectedPoints.description: Explains that real organisations can combine controls and feedback practices.

followUps.prompt: How would the process change if the regulated project also had rapidly changing requirements?

hu.title: SDLC model választása
hu.prompt: Hasonlítsd össze a delivery approachot egy stable requirementű regulated system és egy uncertain experimental product esetén.
hu.answerStructureHint: Requirement volatility, compliance, verification, risk és feedback cost alapján válassz.

asd_regulated.label: Controlled verificationt választ regulated workhöz
asd_regulated.description: Sequential planning vagy V-model traceabilityt és verificationt adhat expensive change mellett.

asd_experiment.label: Iterative learninget választ uncertaintyhez
asd_experiment.description: Incremental vagy iterative delivery korán teszteli a product assumptiont.

asd_risk.label: Risket használ decision factorként
asd_risk.description: Nagy high-risk unknown esetén explicit spiral-style risk work kerül elő.

asd_nodogma.label: Elkerüli a false binaryt
asd_nodogma.description: A valós process kontrollt és feedback practice-et kombinálhat.

followUps.asd_follow_change: Hogyan változna a process, ha a regulated project requirementjei is gyorsan változnának?

questions.title: Using AI-generated code responsibly
questions.prompt: An AI tool produces a large feature quickly, but nobody fully understands it. What would you require before release?
questions.answerStructureHint: Keep human ownership, reduce the change to understandable pieces, verify risks and apply normal quality gates.

expectedPoints.label: Requires explainability
expectedPoints.description: Does not ship code the team cannot explain, debug, secure and maintain.

expectedPoints.label: Performs risk-based review
expectedPoints.description: Checks architecture, edge cases, security, performance and dependencies.

expectedPoints.label: Applies guardrails
expectedPoints.description: Requires tests, types, lint, integration checks and safe rollout.

expectedPoints.label: Reduces change size
expectedPoints.description: Splits generated work into reviewable and verifiable pieces.

followUps.prompt: What data-security rules would you apply when giving context to an AI tool?

hu.title: AI-generated code felelős használata
hu.prompt: Egy AI tool gyorsan generál egy nagy feature-t, de senki nem érti teljesen. Mit követelnél release előtt?
hu.answerStructureHint: Human ownership, understandable small pieces, risk verification és normál quality gate.

aar_understand.label: Explainabilityt követel
aar_understand.description: A team által nem érthető, debugolható, secure-ölhető és maintainelhető code nem shipelhető.

aar_review.label: Risk-based reviewt végez
aar_review.description: Architecture, edge case, security, performance és dependency vizsgált.

aar_tests.label: Guardrailt alkalmaz
aar_tests.description: Test, type, lint, integration check és safe rollout szükséges.

aar_slice.label: Csökkenti a change size-t
aar_slice.description: Generated work reviewable és verifiable részekre bomlik.

followUps.aar_follow_data: Milyen data-security szabályt alkalmaznál, amikor contextet adsz egy AI toolnak?
